name: org.agntcy/ui-dev-mcps
description: Curated collection of MCP servers for UI developers: design-file inspection (Figma), image editing automation (Photoshop), React component scaffolding and analysis, and browser end-to-end testing (Playwright). Every member exposes an MCP endpoint so any MCP-compatible agent can consume them uniformly.

data.name: figma-mcp
data.description:
# Figma MCP

MCP server for Figma Dev Mode. Lets agents inspect design files, read variables (design tokens), and export assets so they can translate designs into code deterministically.

## Features
- **Node inspection**: Read frames, components, auto-layout, and constraints.
- **Design tokens**: Enumerate Figma variables and modes.
- **Asset export**: Export nodes as SVG/PNG at multiple scales.

## Getting Started
```
npx -y figma-developer-mcp --figma-api-key=$FIGMA_API_KEY
```

env_vars.name: FIGMA_API_KEY
env_vars.description: Figma personal access token with `file_content:read` and `file_dev_resources:read` scopes.

http_headers.name: Authorization
http_headers.description: OAuth bearer token issued by Figma for the MCP endpoint.

tools.name: get_file
tools.description: Fetch the tree of a Figma file by URL or key.
examples: List top-level frames in file key ABC123

tools.name: get_node
tools.description: Return properties for a specific node (frame, component, instance).
examples: Get the styles for node 12:345 in file ABC123
Show auto-layout settings for the primary button component

tools.name: list_variables
tools.description: Enumerate design tokens (variables) and their modes.
examples: List all color variables in the 'Brand' collection
Get the light/dark mode values for spacing tokens

tools.name: export_asset
tools.description: Export a node as SVG or PNG at one or more scales.
examples: Export the logo as PNG at 1x, 2x, 3x

data.name: photoshop-mcp
data.description:
# Photoshop MCP

MCP server that drives Adobe Photoshop via UXP so agents can open documents, apply actions, batch-export layers, and run non-destructive edits.

## Features
- **Document control**: Open, save, export as PSD/PNG/JPG/WebP.
- **Layer ops**: Toggle visibility, apply masks, run smart-object updates.
- **Actions**: Invoke saved Photoshop Actions with parameters.

## Getting Started
Install the AGNTCY Photoshop MCP UXP plugin, then:
```
npx -y @agntcy/photoshop-mcp
```

env_vars.name: PHOTOSHOP_UXP_PORT
env_vars.description: Local port the Photoshop UXP host listens on (default 8087).

env_vars.name: ADOBE_CLIENT_ID
env_vars.description: Adobe API client id if remote asset fetching is enabled.

tools.name: open_document
tools.description: Open a PSD from a local path or URL and return its layer tree.

tools.name: export_layer
tools.description: Export one or more layers as PNG/JPG/WebP at a target size.
examples: Batch-export all layers whose name starts with 'icon/'

tools.name: apply_action
tools.description: Run a named Photoshop Action against the active document.
examples: Run action 'Add Watermark' on all open documents

data.name: react-mcp
data.description:
# React MCP

MCP server for React component development. Analyzes existing components, generates new ones from prompts or Figma nodes, and inspects hook usage across a repo.

## Features
- **Static analysis**: Parse JSX/TSX, resolve props, list hooks, find dead components.
- **Component generation**: Emit typed function components with Tailwind or CSS Modules.
- **Story scaffolding**: Generate Storybook stories from component signatures.

## Getting Started
```
npx -y @agntcy/react-mcp --root ./src
```

env_vars.name: REACT_MCP_ROOT
env_vars.description: Root directory containing the React source tree (overrides --root).

tools.name: analyze_component
tools.description: Return props, hooks, imports, and child components for a given .tsx/.jsx file.
examples: List every hook used by src/pages/Dashboard.tsx

tools.name: generate_component
tools.description: Generate a typed React component from a natural-language spec or a Figma node id.
examples: Generate a <PricingCard title price cta /> in TypeScript with Tailwind
Generate a component from Figma node 12:345 into src/components/Hero.tsx

tools.name: list_hooks
tools.description: Enumerate custom hooks defined in the repo and their call sites.
examples: List custom hooks and their callers

tools.name: generate_story
tools.description: Scaffold a Storybook CSF3 story for a component.
examples: Generate a story for src/components/Button.tsx with primary and disabled variants

data.name: playwright-mcp
data.description:
# Playwright MCP

Microsoft's Playwright MCP server. Gives agents a controllable Chromium/Firefox/WebKit browser: navigate, snapshot the accessibility tree, click, type, evaluate JavaScript, and record traces.

## Features
- **Accessibility-tree first**: Uses ARIA-based snapshots (no screenshots required).
- **Cross-browser**: Chromium, Firefox, WebKit.
- **Isolated contexts**: One browser context per session by default.

## Getting Started
```
npx -y @playwright/mcp@latest
```

env_vars.name: PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH
env_vars.description: Directory where Playwright browsers are installed.

http_headers.name: Authorization
http_headers.description: Bearer token for the self-hosted Playwright MCP HTTP endpoint.

tools.name: browser_navigate
tools.description: Navigate the active browser tab to a URL.

tools.name: browser_snapshot
tools.description: Return the accessibility-tree snapshot of the current page.
examples: Get the a11y tree of the checkout view

tools.name: browser_click
tools.description: Click an element addressed by role/name from the last snapshot.
examples: Click the link with role 'link' and name 'Docs'

tools.name: browser_type
tools.description: Type text into an input identified by role/name.

tools.name: browser_evaluate
tools.description: Evaluate a JavaScript expression in the page context.

resources.name: local_filesystem
resources.description: Read/write access to the working directory for source files, exported assets, and generated components.

resources.name: figma_api
resources.description: Access to the Figma REST and MCP APIs for file/node/variable reads and asset exports.

resources.name: photoshop_uxp
resources.description: Local UXP bridge to the running Photoshop instance for document and layer operations.

resources.name: browser_control
resources.description: Full control of a Playwright-managed browser (Chromium, Firefox, WebKit).

resources.name: network
resources.description: Outbound HTTP(S) access to arbitrary origins for browser navigation and remote MCP endpoints.
